questions.question: Why is "understand this PDF" harder than plain OCR?
options: The signal lives in layout, tables, figures, handwriting, and typography as well as text — raw OCR dumps the words and loses that an invoice's "Total: $1,245" came from the bottom-right, not a footnote
questions.explanation: Documents are not photos: headers, footnotes, two-column layouts, merged table cells, and title-vs-body typography all carry meaning that pixel-to-text extraction alone discards.

questions.question: What characterized the Era-1 OCR pipeline, and where did it break?
options: PDF → page images → Tesseract text+bboxes → layout analyzer → table recognizer → regex field extraction; works on clean print, breaks on handwriting, skewed scans, complex tables, and non-English scripts — each failure needing a custom exception path
It required cloud APIs for every page
It only worked on scanned books
questions.explanation: TrOCR (2021) upgraded the OCR step itself with a transformer encoder-decoder — a clean win on handwriting and multilingual text — but the brittle multi-stage pipeline remained.

questions.question: What did OCR-free models like Donut and Nougat change?
options: They skip detection entirely and map page pixels DIRECTLY to structured output — Donut (Swin-B encoder-decoder) emits JSON or markdown per task schema; Nougat targets scientific papers, emitting LaTeX with equations and multi-column layout handled
They only output plain unstructured text
questions.explanation: Era 2 (2022-2023) collapsed the pipeline into one model: no OCR, no layout analyzer, no detection — image in, markup out.

questions.question: What are LayoutLMv3's three input streams?
options: Text tokens, layout (bounding-box coordinates), and image patches — trained with unified masking across all three so the model binds words to their positions on the page
questions.explanation: LayoutLM-family models are the layout-aware middle path: they still consume OCR text but fuse it with spatial and visual signals rather than discarding the page geometry.

questions.question: What does the Era-3 "VLM-native" endgame look like?
options: Feed the page image to a frontier VLM at high native resolution (e.g., 2576px) and get structured-markup output for free — no document-specific architecture at all
A separate specialist model per document type
questions.explanation: By 2026 generic VLM capability absorbed the specialist stack. The remaining engineering judgment is task fit: invoices, scientific papers, handwritten forms, and non-Latin receipts may still favor specialists like Donut, Nougat, DocLLM, or PaliGemma 2.
